inputs.prompt: Interview transcript — Dr. Rachel Moore, climate scientist, 22 minutes. Key excerpts: [2:15] 'The data is unambiguous. We have lost 30% of arctic ice mass in the last two decades.' [5:40] 'People ask me if it's too late. I say it's too late for some things and not too late for others. That distinction matters.' [8:20] 'The government's target is meaningless without enforcement. I've said that publicly and I'll say it again.' [12:45] 'My daughter asked me if the world is ending. I told her no, but I couldn't look at her when I said it.' [16:30] Detailed technical explanation of carbon capture methodology. [19:00] 'What frustrates me most is not the science denial — it's the science acceptance followed by inaction.' Package purpose: 2-minute news package about new government climate targets.

Interview with a local restaurant owner, 15 min. For a 90-second package about rising food costs. Key moments: talked about supply chain problems, mentioned specific price increases for ingredients, got emotional about possibly closing.

Interview transcript — MEP Anna Kowalski, 18 minutes. For a 2-minute package on the EU Digital Markets Act enforcement. Key excerpts: [1:30] 'The Digital Markets Act was supposed to be the most ambitious tech regulation in the world. Six months in, not a single fine has been issued.' [4:15] Extended discussion of lobbying from US tech companies. [7:00] 'Brussels writes beautiful regulations. Enforcement is where it always falls apart.' [10:30] 'I met with the CEO of [major tech company] last month. He told me, with a smile, that compliance was his top priority. His lawyers told me something different.' [14:00] Technical discussion of interoperability requirements.
